title: Echinocystis minima
taxonomy: Echinocystis minima is classified within the family Cucurbitaceae. This family comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as gourds or cucurbits. Echinocystis minima is a type of vine native to North America and is also known as the small prickly cucumber or small balsam apple. The plant is a member of the order Cucurbitales, which includes other vining plants like cucumbers and melons. Echinocystis minima is further grouped into the genus Echinocystis, which contains a single species.

intro: Echinocystis minima, also known as the small grapevine or small cucumber, is a perennial vine native to North America that belongs to the Cucurbitaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being.
